items.title: Stripe’s $53 billion PayPal bid is a high-stakes play to own the future of digital payments
items.description: If Stripe acquires PayPal, the real prize could be consumer wallets, stablecoin issuance or the infrastructure powering the next generation of digital payments.

items.title: Visa backs Open USD with new stablecoin platform as Circle faces fresh competition
items.description: Visa launched a stablecoin platform that lets banks and fintechs issue, manage and settle digital dollars through its payments network.

items.title: Keyrock acquires BlockFills trading assets to expand institutional crypto business
items.description: The deal adds institutional trading clients, derivatives expertise and regulatory licenses as Keyrock looks to deepen its presence in crypto capital markets.

items.title: The Clarity Act is the most important consumer protection effort in years
items.description: Consumers should not have to wait for another crisis to get the protections they deserve, argues Summer Mersinger, CEO of the Blockchain Association.

items.title: Crypto for Advisors: Strengthening defenses against AI fraud
items.description: Strengthening defenses against AI fraud

items.title: Crypto brokerage firm Alpaca raises $135 million for tokenized stock infrastructure
items.description: The company has previously cleared or held in custody roughly 94% of tokenized U.S. equities and now holds over $1.5 billion in underlying stocks.

items.title: Ledger wants AI agents to manage crypto without holding your keys
items.description: AI agents can read wallet balances and analyze portfolios but requires every sensitive action to be approved on a Ledger hardware device before it can be executed.

items.title: Galaxy targets institutional stablecoin yield with new DeFi vaults
items.description: The crypto financial services firm launched Galaxy Curator, a Morpho-based platform that gives Fireblocks' 2,400 institutional clients access to onchain yield strategies.

items.title: Morgan Stanley’s E*TRADE launches spot crypto trading through Zero Hash
items.description: Eligible E*TRADE retail clients can now buy, sell and hold Bitcoin, Ether and Solana through a partnership with crypto infrastructure provider Zero Hash.

items.title: Tradable’s $1B Stellar deal adds to institutional tokenization boom
items.description: Tradable will bring up to $1 billion in private credit assets to Stellar, extending the blockchain’s push into institutional finance and tokenized real-world assets.

items.title: Alpaca raises $135M to fund tokenized agent-first infrastructure
items.description: The BNP-backed brokerage infrastructure provider is expanding into tokenized markets and AI-native financial services as both DeFi and TradFi companies pursue onchain business.

items.title: Gambling on random Pokémon cards: Onchain gagcha hits record high as crypto sinks
items.description: Users spent a record $324 million on onchain gacha in June, even as Bitcoin hit a 21-month low. The thrill of scoring a top Pokemon card from a random pack is becoming big business

items.title: Prediction markets defy crypto downturn with record Q2 volume: CoinGecko
items.description: Prediction markets reached a record $113.8 billion in notional volume in Q2 as spot CEX trading, derivatives volume and stablecoin market cap declined.

items.title: MoonPay buys Glide to bolster crypto deposits infrastructure
items.description: MoonPay acquired Glide, a crypto infrastructure startup founded by former Robinhood Wallet engineers, to expand cross-chain crypto deposit infrastructure.

items.title: Tokenized stocks market cap gains to record $2.3B
items.description: The market capitalization of tokenized stocks rose to a record $2.3 billion, as investors sought exposure to the growing list of tokenized equity products launched by cryptocurrency exchanges.

items.title: FATF urges faster crypto AML enforcement as stablecoin crime increases
items.description: The global task force said criminal networks are using stablecoins and developing proprietary tokens to evade asset freezes as countries struggle to enforce crypto AML rules.

items.title: U.S. grocery slowdown deepens as shoppers buy fewer items, raising pressure on food companies
items.description: New data shows sales declining, and the trend is forcing grocers and brands to compete harder on price and value.

items.title: Trump teleprompter operator allegedly made Kalshi bets on president's statements; CFTC investigating
items.description: Kalshi retained most of the profits, which were more than $90,000, from the trades made on public statements by President Trump.

items.title: Sen. Warren says Trump's CFPB overhaul has cost Americans $26.5 billion
items.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren says the Trump administration's rollback of CFPB rules and enforcement has cost Americans up to $26.5 billion so far.

items.title: Here's what to expect when Netflix reports earnings after the bell
items.description: Investors will be looking for updates on Netflix's ad-supported business, engagement metrics and how the streaming giant continues to consider potential M&A.

items.title: As prediction markets boom, questions arise over who will be the watchdog
items.description: As new contract types are introduced, prediction markets may have to work under two regulatory bodies.

items.title: Short sellers load up against SpaceX as stock drops below IPO price
items.description: About 185 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 29% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

items.title: The best states for workers to get educated, trained and find a job in America in 2026
items.description: These 11 U.S. states excel when it comes to the education level of their workforce, migration patterns, and workforce development programs.

items.title: IRS tax liens can be a 'kiss of death,' consumer advocate says — and they're on the rise
items.description: Federal tax liens filed by the Internal Revenue Service can impact people's employment and their ability to access credit.

Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X6sg439Rilk! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. items.title: Americans say they need $1.2 million to retire. Most are too deep in debt to get there.
items.description: More than 80% of Americans worry about running out of money in retirement: “It really keeps them up at night.”

items.title: This isn’t a plan to save Social Security. It’s a plan to have a plan.
items.description: A bipartisan group of senators, most of whom are retiring, is trying to move the ball forward just a little.

items.title: ‘I don’t want to sound callous’: Why base your Social Security claim on life expectancy? We could die at any time.
items.description: “The actual date of death ultimately affects only the surviving beneficiaries.”

items.title: Is SpaceX’s stock a bust because it fell below $135? Look what happened after Meta’s IPO.
items.description: Close to half of major IPOs sink below their offering price — and stay there for several years. Here’s why SpaceX is no different.

items.title: Mortgage rates jump to the highest level of 2026
items.description: Mortgage rates edged up to the highest level in a year, delivering a fresh blow to the housing market.

items.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
items.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

items.title: AST SpaceMobile’s stock is sinking as the SpaceX rival looks to raise more cash
items.description: AST SpaceMobile also pushed back a satellite-launch target.

items.title: The No. 1 decision for aging retirees: Stay at home or move into a senior community?
items.description: Why staying in your longtime home requires more planning than you think.

2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Zelenskyy’s government plunged into turmoil by defence minister’s firing
items.description: Ukrainian president forced into concession after ousting of popular minister prompts public and political backlash

items.title: Ex-motorway boss handed 12-year prison term over Genoa bridge disaster
items.description: Former chief executive of Autostrade per l’Italia convicted for his role in 2018 tragedy in north-west Italy

items.title: Chevron and Iraq seek to bypass Strait of Hormuz with Syria pipeline
items.description: Consortium backing project also includes Syrian-Qatari group and LA venture firm with ties to Trump ally Tom Barrack

items.title: Russian strikes threaten Ukraine’s Black Sea grain trade
items.description: Wheat prices have surged as heavy attacks disrupt shipments

items.title: Uber’s super app ‘synergies’ deserve some suspension of disbelief
items.description: Ride-hailing giant’s purchase of Delivery Hero may be more appetising than it looks

items.title: TfL hackers ‘central’ to Scattered Spider group, says NCA
items.description: Two men each sentenced to five years and six months in prison for 2024 cyber attack

items.title: Varun Chandra to stay as Number 10 business adviser under Burnham
items.description: Former Hakluyt managing partner was instrumental in securing a US-UK trade deal

items.title: We need humanities more than ever in the age of AI
items.description: Subjects like literature, languages and history are vital to a thoughtful and prosperous society

items.title: Mary-Dell Chilton Dies at 87; Helped Create First Genetically Modified Plant
items.description: In 1982, she led the research team that figured out how to genetically alter plants, a discovery that would eventually transform global agriculture.

items.title: White House Faces Stiff Pushback on Subjecting Grants to Political Review
items.description: Academics, city leaders and congressional lawmakers number among the thousands to urge the Trump administration to reconsider a plan to assert more control over grants.

items.title: Americans Are Spending, and Not Just on Necessities
items.description: Lower gas prices have been a relief for households struggling with higher living costs. But now they are creeping higher again.

items.title: Maker of Drone Boats Used in Iran War Will Open New Texas Shipyard
items.description: Saronic Technologies will make naval and commercial vessels at the facility. The company’s naval drone helped rescue the crew of a downed Apache helicopter last month.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Tanker Traffic Erodes Further as Oil Prices Rise
items.description: Very few ships passed through the waterway on the first full day of the U.S. naval blockade of Iran.

items.title: Anthropic Inches Toward a Mega-I.P.O.
items.description: The artificial intelligence lab is said to have taken more steps that are consistent with a company aiming to go public in the fall.

items.title: Google Ordered to Give A.I. Rivals More Access on Android Smartphones
items.description: The decision by European Union regulators is a response to fears that Google will use its vast Android user base to gain an edge in A.I.

items.title: U.K. Nationalizes British Steel, Its Last Major Steel Mill
items.description: The government first intervened in British Steel last year to prevent its then-owner, the Chinese company Jingye Group, from shutting it down. No private sector buyers came forward.
